data.title: Covenant AI's exit caused TAO's stock to plummet 17%, signaling a crisis for Bittensor.
data.description:
Tao Zhu, Jinse Finance

Summary

On April 9, 2026, Covenant AI, the main subnet developer on Bittensor, announced its withdrawal from the Bittensor network ecosystem. Covenant claims that Bittensor co-founder Jacob Steeves has control over the network and accuses Bittensor's promise of decentralization of being "a lie."

Why is Covenant AI leaving Bittensor? How did Bittensor respond? Why did Covenant AI's withdrawal cause Bittensor to plummet by 17%?

I. Covenant AI's Statement

Covenant AI founder Sam Dare issued a statement on X, pointing out that the reason for Covenant AI's departure from Bittensor is the centralized power of Bittensor co-founder Jacob Steeves. The three-way alliance structure (multi-signature for network upgrades by three people, claiming to the community that this is distributed governance) is just a show of decentralization.

Why is Covenant AI leaving Bittensor?

Why is Covenant AI leaving Bittensor?

I. Covenant AI's Statement

Covenant AI founder Sam Dare issued a statement on X, pointing out that the reason for Covenant AI's departure from Bittensor is the centralized power of Bittensor co-founder Jacob Steeves. The three-way alliance structure (multi-signature for network upgrades by three people, claiming to the community that this is distributed governance) is just a show of decentralization.

On April 9, 2026, Covenant AI announced its withdrawal from Bittensor due to the centralized power of Bittensor co-founder Jacob Steeves.

... Jacob Steeves effectively controls the three-person governing team, capable of unilaterally deploying changes at any time without any procedures or consensus, with the other members acting as legal shields. The specific statement is as follows: Covenant AI is built on a simple belief: the training of cutting-edge AI models should not be controlled by any single entity. Over the past two years, our team has delivered on this vision. Covenant-72B—a model with 72 billion parameters, collaboratively trained by over 70 independent contributors using general-purpose hardware in a permissionless environment—is arguably the largest decentralized large-scale language model (LLM) pre-training project in history. It not only garnered recognition from Nvidia's CEO and was cited by Anthropic's co-founder, but it also propelled the ecosystem we built to achieve a value surge of up to 90%. We never sought attention intentionally. We simply wanted to prove that decentralized training models work. The attention that followed was merely a testament to the power of our work. We hope to provide an unambiguous and clear explanation of what all this means. When a single entity can arbitrarily halt the token distribution of a subnet; can override the owner and forcibly take over its management of its own community space; can blatantly abandon a specific project without any established procedures; and can even distort token sales into a coercive mechanism to force others to comply—this is not true "decentralization." It is merely "centralized control" disguised as "decentralization." Every participant, miner, validator, and investor in this ecosystem must be keenly aware that: This totalitarian power truly exists; it has been exercised by Jacob Steeves (i.e., Const); and its purpose is not to maintain the healthy development of the network, but to re-establish control over a team—a team that has become increasingly independent and difficult to manage. For those whose power is entirely based on the fact that "everyone depends on him," any subnet owner capable of independently building communities, making independent decisions, and operating freely without permission is undoubtedly a potential threat. The problems of decentralization are far more complex than any single event can suggest. Bittensor employs a "three-way alliance" operational architecture: three specific individuals jointly manage multisignature (multisig) permissions related to network upgrades; however, when promoting this architecture to the community, it is embellished as a kind of "distributed governance" model. This is far from the truth. It's nothing more than a clumsy "decentralization" political show. Jacob Steeves actually maintains firm control over the real power of this "three-way alliance," vehemently resisting any substantial decentralization and unilaterally pushing for changes anytime, anywhere—without any established procedures or consensus. As for the nominal members within it, their only value is to act as legal "shields"—in the event of accountability or litigation, they will be the first to bear legal responsibility, while Jacob himself will remain unscathed. This online community talks a lot about so-called "governance" and "decentralization," but has never put them into practice. The reins of power have never left the hands of any one person. This is the crux of the problem. Bittensor's entire core premise—the promise that attracted developers, miners, validators, and investors to this ecosystem—is that no single entity can control it. However, this promise is actually a lie. We cannot continue building on a network where our fundamental promise to investors—that the infrastructure is decentralized and permissionless—is at odds with the reality of its actual governance. We cannot raise funds, recruit talent, or ask the community to contribute resources on a foundation that can be arbitrarily destroyed by anyone. We absolutely do not want to pass this risk on to those who trust us. Therefore, with deep regret and frustration, we hereby announce that Covenant AI is officially withdrawing from the Bittensor network. Over the past few weeks, Jacob Steeves (also known as "Const") has taken a series of actions against the operations of Covenant AI that fundamentally contradict the principles the network claims to uphold. These actions include: suspending the distribution of token rewards (Emissions) to our subnets; stripping us of administrative privileges within our own community channels; unilaterally abandoning our subnet infrastructure; and exerting direct economic pressure at a critical moment in our operational conflict through a large-scale, high-profile token sell-off. These are by no means governance decisions made through transparent, decentralized consensus mechanisms. They are merely punitive retaliations by one person—who has never truly relinquished control of the network, despite having publicly declared to the world that he was no longer responsible for its operation. Covenant AI's mission has never changed. Decentralized, permissionless AI training is not a feature unique to Bittensor; it's a technological capability our team is passionately working to advance. Our research, our team members, our model assets, and our grand vision will all go with us. We are currently working on a series of exciting projects and initiatives, and related news and announcements will be officially released to the public soon. To our community members, mining partners, and every supporter who has contributed computing power, time, and faith to the Covenant-72B project: you have proven that things once deemed "impossible" are indeed entirely achievable. The proof of this great achievement does not reside on a single Discord server, nor is it dependent on the governance architecture of any particular network; it is deeply rooted in our research, our AI models, and the very soul of our team. Wherever we choose our next base of operations, we will continue to work diligently to earn your trust and support. Decentralization is not merely a marketing slogan—it will not be allowed to be arbitrarily overturned or trampled upon when this concept becomes "out of place" or "obstructive" for Const. It is a solemn commitment to every builder, every miner, and every investor—they entered this ecosystem based on the promise that "no individual could possibly do what we are doing now." Either make this system truly decentralized, or stop pretending it is. Sam Dare, Founder of Covenant AI (blockquote) II. Response from Bittensor Co-founder Jacob Steeves (blockquote) Jacob Steeves did not initially respond directly, only saying: This will mark the birth of the first subnets on Bittensor, which will operate in a "headless" mode and exist as a true commodity. Following this, Steeves continued: Exploitations can teach a system about its weaknesses. The faster you discover them, the faster you learn. Tonight's achievement is that Bittensor will invent a locking-based subnet ownership mechanism—specifically, subnet ownership depends on the team's long-term financial investment in the project. This means: 1) Investors can see well in advance whether the owners have unlocked their tokens; 2) They can reprice subnets before the owners; 3) They can flexibly transfer their funds to another team or agency to manage the system. Thanks to @DistStateAndMe for helping to further advance Bittensor's decentralization and developing a solution to one of the oldest problems in the cryptocurrency space: founders deceiving token holders. Looking forward to training some 1T parameter models with expert miners in the field. "The dead never rise again." III. Impact on Bittensor Before its withdrawal, Covenant AI was widely regarded as one of the key growth engines of the Bittensor ecosystem. The Covenant-72B model achieved a major breakthrough: a large language model with 72 billion parameters; collaboratively trained by 70+ independent contributors; completed on general-purpose hardware in a permissionless environment. It was hailed as one of the largest decentralized LLM pre-training experiments in history. Specifically, training a 72 billion parameter model typically requires a tightly coupled cluster of high-end GPUs connected by InfiniBand—hardware that costs tens of millions of dollars and requires latency between nodes to be below milliseconds. Traditionally, it was believed that ordinary internet connections could never bridge this gap. Covenant-72B directly challenges this view, employing SparseLoCo technology—which combines sparsity, 2-bit quantization, and error feedback—resulting in over 146 times less inter-node communication compared to previous distributed operations. The result is that Bittensor's Templar subnet, trained on over 70 peer nodes using 1.1 trillion tokens, and validated using the Gauntlet system, which rewards high-quality nodes (TAO) and penalizes poorly performing nodes using loss assessment and OpenSkill ranking. The endorsement from prominent figures like Jensen Huang and Chamas Palihapitia boosted Bittensor's credibility and strengthened investor confidence in the ecosystem. Therefore, Covenant AI is one of the core narrative drivers in the TAO surge. However, as the event unfolded, the most significant impact on Bittensor was reflected in the market. Following the announcement, the TAO price plummeted and has not yet shown a significant rebound as of this writing. It closed at $264.09, down 17.7%.

Furthermore, as mentioned in Covenant AI, Bittensor suffers from a false sense of decentralization. Therefore, it remains to be seen whether developers and investors will lose confidence in Bittensor. For a long time, Bittensor has been tied to the core narrative of decentralized AI training, and Covenant AI's exit directly impacts the future prospects of this narrative.

Some observers on X pointed out:

  • Will the future of TAO tokens gradually lead to zero due to internal power struggles and divisions within the project team?

    In addition, as mentioned in Covenant AI, Bittensor suffers from a false sense of decentralization.

    Will the future of TAO tokens gradually lead to zero?

    How would you handle the power distribution between subnet owners and the entire ecosystem? I don't care about decentralization: I only care about price.

  • So Sam Dare is the Sam Bankman-Fried of the Bittensor ecosystem.

  • A silly question. Given this governance issue, isn't now the best time to focus on subnets governed by agents rather than humans?

  • The short-term pain of $TAO will eventually bring long-term benefits.

  • We need to fundamentally increase the cost of malicious exits, reduce discontent through legitimate governance, and build a strong enough sense of platform identity so that leaving the platform is a real loss.

Conclusion

In this debate, the concept of decentralization contradicts actual power: Covenant AI expressed its disagreement with centralized power and chose to eventually exit; while Jacob Steeves emphasized mechanism evolution and downplayed the power issue.

In the short term, the market has already reflected this uncertainty in its prices; however, in the long term, the key variable determining TAO's value lies in whether Bittensor can restore governance trust, attract new high-quality subnets, and maintain its decentralized AI narrative.

data.title: Coinbase Partners MarketVector To Launch New Index Combining BTC And Tokenised Gold For Institutional Investors
data.description:
Bitcoin And Tokenised Gold Meet In New Store-Of-Value Benchmark

MarketVector Indexes and Coinbase Asset Management have launched a new benchmark that combines Bitcoin with tokenised gold, aiming to track how investors are thinking about capital preservation in a period of heavy debt and persistent inflation pressure.

The new Coinbase Store of Value Index is built around BTC and Pax Gold (PAXG), one of the largest gold-backed tokens.

It is designed as a price-return index in US dollars and uses a rules-based approach rather than a fixed split between the two assets.

Coinbase and MarketVector launched the Coinbase Store of Value Index, tracking Bitcoin and tokenized gold. It uses a dynamic, rules-based allocation method, adjusting weights based on relative volatility to balance Bitcoin's growth potential with gold's historical stability. pic.twitter.com/YXRkT94bU1

— Bitcoin Bandit (@Birchley23) April 10, 2026

Why The Index Looks Beyond Gold Alone

Gold has long been seen as a safe place for wealth, but Bitcoin is increasingly being treated in a similar way by some investors.

The idea behind the index is to reflect that shift, especially as demand grows for scarce assets that are not tied to government debt or money printing.

Anthony Bassili, President at Coinbase Asset Management, said:

“We believe this index represents a meaningful evolution in store-of-value investing. In a world of fiscal dominance, the future belongs to scarce assets that cannot be printed and are not an obligation of a government or private issuer.”

Gold has long defined “store of value," but that definition is evolving. We’ve partnered with @CoinbaseAM to launch $COINSOV, a rules-based benchmark combining Bitcoin + gold in a volatility-aware framework. Built to help investors navigate today’s macro environment.
Learn more:… pic.twitter.com/AoggNRjwDm

— MarketVector Indexes (@MarketVector) April 9, 2026

How The Allocation Changes

The index does not hold Bitcoin and gold at fixed weights.

Instead, it uses inverse volatility to guide allocation, giving more weight to the asset that has been less volatile at the time.

It is rebalanced every quarter, allowing the mix to adjust as market conditions change.

MarketVector said its research, covering 2017 through 2025, suggests the model delivered better risk-adjusted returns than simpler approaches.

It also showed smaller drawdowns than a basic 50/50 allocation, while outperforming several widely followed benchmarks over the same period.

Why Institutions May Pay Attention

The launch comes as Bitcoin’s store-of-value case faces more scrutiny.

Over the past year, it has often traded more like a risk asset, moving alongside equities at times.

That has prompted some investors to question whether it still behaves like digital gold in all market conditions.

Martin Leinweber, Director of Digital Asset Research and Strategy at MarketVector, said the index reflects rising demand for hybrid investment solutions as macro uncertainty deepens.

Major signal from the indexing world.

MarketVector, the same company that runs the MarketVector Digital Assets 100 Index, just launched the Coinbase Store of Value Index.

It tracks Bitcoin and tokenized gold together.

Not Bitcoin and tech stocks. Not Bitcoin and crypto… pic.twitter.com/DSIGCmSq5P

— Taurus (@Taurus4BTC) April 10, 2026

MarketVector, which is based in Europe and acts as a regulated benchmark administrator, has already expanded beyond traditional indexing into digital assets with products such as the MarketVector Digital Assets 100 Index and the Coinbase 50 Index.

The new benchmark is meant to sit at the crossroads of old and new stores of value, giving institutions a structured way to combine Bitcoin’s upside with gold’s steadier profile.

data.title: Trump’s Mar-a-Lago Memecoin Gala Clashes With Correspondents’ Dinner—Can He Be in Two Places at Once?
data.description:
Will The Mar-a-Lago Gala Be Cancelled Due To Trump's Absence?

A high-profile memecoin gala hosted by Donald Trump at Mar-a-Lago is raising fresh doubts over both scheduling feasibility and ethics, as the event clashes with the White House Correspondents’ Dinner and draws scrutiny from U.S. lawmakers over potential conflicts of interest.

Trump is set to host an exclusive luncheon for top holders of his Official TRUMP memecoin at his Florida estate on April 25—the same day he is expected to attend the White House Correspondents’ Dinner in Washington, D.C.

ABD’de $Trump bağlantılı memecoin için 25 Nisan’da Mar-a-Lago’da özel bir etkinlik düzenlenecek.

Etkinlik, en büyük 297 yatırımcıya açık olacak ve Trump’ın konuşmacı olarak yer alacağı şekilde tanıtılıyor.

Ancak Trump’ın programa katılıp katılmayacağı net değil.

Aynı gün… pic.twitter.com/IpZKpksiWi

— Coinkolik (@Coinkolik) April 9, 2026

The gala is marketed as an all-day experience beginning in the morning, featuring a VIP reception and champagne toast with Trump for the top 29 token holders. Invitations are tied to a leaderboard ranking participants based on their time-weighted holdings, effectively linking token ownership to access.

However, a disclosure published on the event's website that stated "Trump might not be able to attend the conference" has raised eyebrows, with some speculating that the event itself could even be canceled or rescheduled.

In such cases, participants could instead receive a limited-edition TRUMP-themed NFT, raising further questions about the reliability of the promised in-person access.

Despite the apparent scheduling conflict, organizers have maintained that Trump’s attendance at both engagements is expected.

"Of course, the President will be attending both events. After the conference at Mar-A-Largo, the President will fly back to Washington for the White House Correspondents' dinner."

UPDATE: Donald Trump faces a scheduling clash as his $TRUMP memecoin gala at Mar-a-Lago coincides with the White House Correspondents’ Dinner, raising questions over attendance and event plans.

Politics meets crypto again. What’s your take on this crossover?…

— Roundtable Network (@RTB_io) April 9, 2026

Trump had previously confirmed his participation at the correspondents dinner, which would mark his first appearance after boycotting the event throughout his first term. Still, the tight turnaround between Florida and Washington has fueled skepticism, particularly given Trump’s brief appearance at a similar memecoin-linked gala in 2025.

That earlier event, held at a Trump golf property, drew protests and political backlash, with Trump reportedly staying for only about 20 minutes—adding to doubts about how much access token holders may realistically receive.

Lawmakers question whether memecoin is “selling access”

Beyond logistics, the event has triggered a sharper political response. Senators Elizabeth Warren, Adam Schiff, and Richard Blumenthal have raised concerns that the memecoin project may be leveraging Trump’s appearance to drive demand for the token.

🇺🇲 SEN. ELIZABETH WARREN TARGETS TRUMP-BACKED $WLFI, CALLS IT A MAJOR CORRUPTION SCANDAL 🚨

“President Trump’s crypto company is the most disgraceful presidential corruption scandal in history.”

She also warned:
“Anyone who owns 10% or more of World Liberty Financial must be… pic.twitter.com/tUA842kQPV

— COACHTY (@TheRealTRTalks) February 27, 2026

In a letter to promoter Bill Zanker, the lawmakers questioned whether organizers were effectively “dangling access” to the president to encourage purchases that could generate fees benefiting Trump and his associates—particularly given the uncertainty around his attendance.

The scrutiny comes amid broader tensions in Washington over crypto regulation and ethics, with lawmakers increasingly focused on how digital asset ventures intersect with political influence and financial gain.

The controversy has unfolded alongside renewed volatility in the TRUMP token. Following the announcement of the second gala in March, the token surged from roughly $2.70 to as high as $4.40 before settling back near the $3 range.

The structure of the event—where higher token holdings translate into closer proximity to Trump—has intensified criticism that financial participation is being directly tied to political access.

As questions mount, the memecoin gala is emerging as a flashpoint in the evolving relationship between politics and crypto markets. Whether Trump ultimately attends both events or not, the combination of scheduling uncertainty, financial incentives, and ethical concerns is likely to keep the spotlight firmly on the initiative.

data.title: DeepSeek V4: Everything You Need to Know About the New Trillion-Parameter AI Model
data.description:
Source: DeepSeek Official Website, Compiled by: Jinse Finance

The field of artificial intelligence is undergoing a major transformation. DeepSeek V4 is DeepSeek's upcoming flagship model, which not only breaks through the boundaries of parameter scale but also achieves unprecedented high efficiency. This model is expected to have 1 trillion (1T) parameters, natively support multimodal operation, and is equipped with 1 million token context windows, directly competing with products from Western tech giants such as OpenAI's GPT-5.4 and Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.5.

In this comprehensive analysis, we will delve into the core specifications, architectural innovations, expected pricing, and the strategic hardware deployment shift behind DeepSeek V4.

In this comprehensive analysis, we will analyze the core specifications, architectural innovations, expected pricing, and the strategic hardware deployment shift behind DeepSeek V4.

Key Highlights

  • Adopts MoE architecture, trillion-parameter scale, but only activates approximately 32-37 billion parameters when processing each token

  • 1 million token context windows, equivalent to the text volume of 15-20 novels

  • Native multimodal capabilities: supports text, image, video, and audio processing from the underlying layer

  • API pricing is only 1/10 to 1/50 of GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.5

  • Model weights are expected to be based on Apache 2.0

  • Open source license. Local deployment and operation are supported by dual RTX 4090 graphics cards or a single RTX 5090 graphics card. What is DeepSeek V4? Building on the success of its predecessors (such as V3 and R1), DeepSeek V4 combines massive scalability with extreme cost-effectiveness. The following are its main technical parameters:

    Three Breakthrough Architectural Innovations

    The true strength of DeepSeek V4 lies not only in its powerful computing power, but also in three specific architectural breakthroughs that make training and running trillion-parameter models possible and cost-controllable.

    1. Engram Conditional Memory

    The biggest challenge faced by a million-token context window is accurately retrieving information without a sharp increase in computing costs. DeepSeek solves this problem through the Engram architecture.

    This system separates static facts (such as API signatures or specific patterns) from dynamic reasoning. In the "Needle-in-a-Haystack" benchmark (finding specific facts among 1 million tokens), Enggram dramatically improved accuracy from a normal 84.2% to an astonishing 97%. 2. Manifold-Constrained Hyperconnected Structures (mHC) As AI models grow in size, they often experience training instability issues (such as gradient explosion). Manifold-constrained hyperconnected structures (mHC) are a mathematical framework that constrains the amplification of signals, keeping it within 2x (compared to up to 3000x in the unconstrained state). This allows DeepSeek to stably train a trillion-parameter model with only 6.7% of the computational cost. 3. DeepSeek DSA Mechanism and Lightning Indexer To efficiently handle the context of 1 million tokens, V4 uses a sparse attention mechanism (DSA) instead of traditional dense attention. The Lightning Indexer can quickly scan the context and locate relevant fragments, and the model only needs to perform attention computation on these specific tokens. In long context scenarios, this solution can reduce computational cost by approximately 50%. Benchmark Comparison: DeepSeek V4 vs GPT-5.4 and Claude 4.5 DeepSeek V4 focuses on software engineering and deep inference capabilities. According to internal benchmark data, the model exhibits outstanding performance. Note: This data is currently based on leaked internal information and will require independent third-party verification after the model's official release. API Pricing: Cutting-Edge AI with the Best Cost-Effectiveness

    Western models such as GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus offer powerful performance, but are expensive to use. For example, GPT-5.2/5.4 costs approximately $1.75 to $15.00 per million input tokens, while Claude Opus 4.5 costs $5.00 per million.

    API Pricing: Cutting-Edge AI with the Best Cost-Effectiveness

    Western models such as GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.5 cost approximately $1.75 to $15.00 per million input tokens.

    API Pricing: Cutting-Edge AI with the Best Cost-Effectiveness

    Western models such as GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.5 cost approximately $ ...1.75 to $15.00 per million input tokens, while Claude Opus 4.5 costs approximately $5.00 per million input tokens.

    API Pricing: Cutting-Edge AI with the Best Cost-Effectiveness

    Western models such as GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.5 cost approximately $1.75 to $15.00 per million input tokens, The expected API pricing for DeepSeek V4 is highly disruptive: This means that, with comparable performance, DeepSeek V4 will cost approximately 1/10 to 1/50 of its Western competitors. Geopolitics and Hardware: Shifting to the Huawei Ecosystem One of the most far-reaching features of DeepSeek V4 lies in its hardware support system. Due to strict US export controls on NVIDIA's high-end GPUs (such as the B300 and H200), DeepSeek has deeply optimized V4, making its inference process primarily rely on domestically produced Chinese chips. While the initial training phase of the model likely still uses NVIDIA hardware (such as the H800), the model has been highly optimized for Huawei's Ascend 950PR and Cambricon's MLU chip. Reportedly, the computing performance of Huawei's Ascend 950PR is 2.87 times that of NVIDIA's H20 (currently a chip legally allowed to be exported to China). This marks a significant milestone for China in achieving self-sufficiency in AI chips. Release Date: When will DeepSeek V4 be released? DeepSeek V4 has experienced several delays. Initially rumored to launch in mid-February 2026 during the Lunar New Year, the release was delayed due to significant engineering challenges in training on the new hardware architecture. On March 9, 2026, an unreleased "V4 Lite" version (approximately 200 billion parameters) appeared on the DeepSeek platform, effectively validating the feasibility of its core architecture. It is now widely expected that the full trillion-parameter version will be officially released in April 2026. Following release, DeepSeek is expected to release the model weights under the Apache 2.0 open-source license. Thanks to the efficiency of the MoE architecture and quantization techniques (INT8/INT4), this ultra-large-scale model is expected to run natively on consumer-grade hardware, such as dual RTX 4090 graphics cards or a single RTX 5090 graphics card.

    DeepSeek V4 Frequently Asked Questions

    1. Is DeepSeek V4 better than ChatGPT (GPT-5.4)?

    According to internal benchmark results, in complex software engineering tasks (SWE-bench) and codebase-level programming tasks, DeepSeek V4 performs comparably to GPT-5.4 and Claude 4.5, and even slightly outperforms them, especially with its million-token context window. However, after the model is officially released, independent testing is still needed to determine the absolute winner.

    2. Can I run DeepSeek V4 locally?

    Yes, provided that the model weights are open-sourced as planned.

    Because the MoE architecture activates only about 32 billion parameters at a time, the quantized version (INT4) can theoretically run locally on a single 32GB RTX 5090 or dual RTX 4090 graphics cards. 3. Why is DeepSeek V4 so low-cost? Its low cost stems from the efficient MoE architecture (activating only a portion of the model's parameters), low training costs (estimated at around $10 million, while similar Western models cost over $100 million), and the application of the DeepSeek DSA mechanism—a technology that halves the computational power required for long contexts. 4. What does V4's so-called "native multimodal" mean? Unlike earlier models that used a "late-stage fusion" approach by "stitching" visual plugins onto text models, V4 is jointly trained from the outset on text, images, video, and audio. This gives it superior cross-modal reasoning capabilities, such as seamlessly generating corresponding interface code directly from hand-drawn architecture diagrams. Conclusion: DeepSeek V4 is not just an incremental update, but a breakthrough at the architectural level. It proves that intelligent optimization (Engram memory architecture, mHC hyperconnection structure, DSA mechanism) can completely rival high-cost, brute-force parameter scaling methods. With its native multimodal capabilities, million-token context window, and disruptive pricing strategy, DeepSeek is forcing enterprises and independent developers to re-evaluate their AI infrastructure. Once the API and open-source weights are launched as planned in April 2026, the overall landscape of artificial intelligence development may undergo a fundamental transformation.

data.title: Liang Wenfeng revealed internally that DeepSeek V4 will be released in late April.
data.description:
Author: Zhang Chi, Source: Phoenix Technology "Innovation Notes"

After several rumors of delays, the highly anticipated DeepSeek V4 is about to be released.

According to "Innovation Notes" from multiple sources, DeepSeek founder Liang Wenfeng recently revealed in an internal communication that DeepSeek's new flagship large model, DeepSeek V4, will be officially released in late April.

Previously, reports indicated that DeepSeek V4 would not only bring trillions of parameters and millions of context windows, but also achieve deep compatibility with domestically produced chips such as Huawei's Ascend for the first time, marking a crucial step forward for China's AI industry on the path of "de-CUDAization." It's worth noting that on the evening of March 29th, DeepSeek experienced a full 13-hour service outage. At the time, the official explanation was only "server failure," but many developers discovered significant changes in model output logic after the service was restored: the coding structure was clearer, the logical rigor was dramatically improved, and the SVG graphics generation capability was significantly enhanced. Industry insiders generally speculate that this "shutdown" was actually a gray-scale test or full deployment of V4. It is understood that V4 will be open-sourced under the Apache 2.0 license, allowing enterprises to deploy it independently. According to the latest foreign media reports, in preparation for the V4 release, domestic tech giants such as Alibaba, ByteDance, and Tencent have pre-ordered hundreds of thousands of next-generation AI computing chips, planning to provide DeepSeek's new models through cloud services and integrate them into their own AI products. As a result, the price of the new AI chips has recently increased by about 20%. Previously, the release date of DeepSeek had been delayed multiple times. Originally planned for a high-parameter version around mid-February, before the Spring Festival, it was postponed due to reasons such as deep adaptation to domestic computing power, architecture restructuring, and stability refinement. Liang Wenfeng, who transitioned from quantitative investment to AI, is undergoing his own "transformation." According to LatePost, although some core members of the DeepSeek team left in the past year (including R1 core author Guo Daya), "some left, but more stayed," and the team did not suffer a complete loss. Since the second half of 2025, Liang Wenfeng has begun to emphasize productization and commercialization more, building a product team, recruiting "model strategy product managers" specializing in Agents, and extending from pure model development to the application layer. Simultaneously, he is valuing the company to clarify the value of employee stock options and provide the team with more certain expectations. Facing strong competition from rivals such as Doubao, Qianwen, Zhipu, Kimi, and MiniMax, the V4, released several months late, carries immense pressure and expectations—can Liang Wenfeng once again create the world's best open-source large model? It is understood that V4's biggest technological highlight lies in its breakthrough in LTM (Long Term Memory) capabilities. However, several AI entrepreneurs who have had in-depth collaborations with DeepSeek told *Chuangzhi Ji* that the industry should not have too high expectations; V4 is unlikely to replicate the influence of DeepSeek-V3 last year. In addition to DeepSeek V4, the Hunyuan 3.0 large-scale model will also be officially released in April. This is Tencent's first major large-scale model product since the team restructuring in the second half of 2025 and the appointment of Yao Shunyu as Chief AI Scientist.
